LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT ROSA GARCIA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF OUR VICTORIAN SCHOOLHOUSE, LLC IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS TIMELY FILED BECAUSE THE TIME RUNS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THE TERMS OF AND PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT. THE COURT OVERRULES AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. IT IS TRUE THAT UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION, BUT PLAINTIFF RAISED THAT ISSUE FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY PAPERS. THE COURT OVERRULES AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION. PARAGRAPHS 27-28 ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT WOULD NOT HAVE ENTERED INTO THE TRANSACTION BUT FOR THE MISTAKE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-27-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-16-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT ROSA GARCIA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF OUR VICTORIAN SCHOOLHOUSE, LLC is overruled in its entirety. The cross-complaint was timely filed because the time runs from notice of entry of order. Causes of action 1 and 2 sufficiently allege the terms of and parties to the contract. The Court overrules as to the 3rd cause of action. It is true that unjust enrichment is not a distinct cause of action, but Plaintiff raised that issue for the first time in the reply papers. The Court overrules as to the 4th cause of action. Paragraphs 27-28 allege that Defendant would not have entered into the transaction but for the mistake. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OUR VICTORIAN SCHOOLHOUSE, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff's allegations of retaliation support the prayer for exemplary damages. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
